attributes.action: Assist in changes to the parking at the Maelor Hospital in Wrexham, NE Wales.
attributes.additional_details: I have personally been in the situation where I have arrived over an hour early for an appointment before 9.00am and the whole parking areas, which is very large, has been totally full. After spending valuable time driving around I have then had to drive away and park in the town and pay to park my car and walk 20 to 30 minutes to try and get to my appointment on time. I have arthritis in my hips and knees and also a heart condition, and this parking issue causes me stress and pain.
attributes.background: I would like this longstanding issue to be discussed by the Welsh Government and to prioritise changes to prevent missed appointments, stress and upset to patients and visitors to the hospital. It is a longstaning problem with no committments by the Trust to make improvements.

rejection.details:
We can’t accept petitions that call for action at a local level.

You could ask your MP to take action on your concerns about this issue.

You can find contact details for your local MP here: https://members.parliament.uk/FindYourMP

You may also wish to raise this issue with your local Members of the Senedd who represent you. You can find out who your Members of the Senedd are and how to contact them here: https://senedd.wales/find-a-member-of-the-senedd/

You could also raise your issue with a local councillor who represents you. You can use this page to find out who your local councillors are, and how to contact them: https://www.gov.uk/find-your-local-councillors
